synthesis.summary: COHR surged 13.3% with no supplied news to explain the move. The elevated relative volume (1.24x) and strong multi-day momentum suggest possible options flow, sector rotation into optical/photonics names, or a catalyst not captured in the provided news set.

Lumentum surged 16.6% after being announced for inclusion in the Nasdaq-100 index, which mechanically forces index-tracking funds to buy the stock, creating immediate demand-driven price pressure. Volume running 57% above average confirms institutional buying consistent with index rebalancing flows. The magnitude of the move is typical for Nasdaq-100 inclusion events given the scale of passive capital that must be deployed.

AAOI surged 24% today with no identifiable news catalyst in the supplied feed. The move could reflect options flow, short squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation, but none of these can be confirmed from available data.

ASPI is up 15% on 3.4x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The catalyst is likely options flow, retail momentum, or sector rotation — none of which can be confirmed from available data.

ASST surged 12.7% on 1.62x relative volume with no identifiable news catalyst in the supplied feed. The move could reflect options flow, short squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation — none of which are supported by available information.

CERT dropped 18.6% on 2.68x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The oversold RSI (29.9), bearish MACD cross, and elevated volume suggest aggressive selling, but the catalyst — whether earnings, a regulatory event, insider activity, or options flow — is not visible in the available news.

DGXX is up 11.65% today and nearly 88% over five days on 3.58x normal volume, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. With RSI at 89.3 and overbought flags triggered, this looks like momentum-driven speculation or options/retail flow — not an identifiable fundamental catalyst.

FCEL surged 16.5% with elevated volume and an RSI of 72.9, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The 5-day gain of ~23% suggests a multi-day momentum run, possibly driven by options flow, short-squeeze dynamics, or sector rotation in fuel cell/clean energy names — none of which can be confirmed from available data.

NRGV is up 11% today on 2.2x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The catalyst is unknown — this could be options flow, short covering, or sector rotation, but none of those mechanisms are evidenced here.

NVTS surged 24.4% today with no supplied news to explain the move. The 5-day gain of 42% and relative volume 2.27x above normal suggest a sustained momentum burst — likely driven by options flow, short covering, or sector rotation — but none of those mechanisms are visible in the available news.

POET is up 26.7% today and nearly 95% over the past five days on 1.47x relative volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The magnitude and duration suggest a catalyst exists — possibly a product announcement, partnership, or options-driven squeeze — but nothing in the available news supports a specific explanation.

Plug Power reported Q1 results that beat analyst estimates, which triggered the initial surge, but the headline itself notes the stock struggled to hold gains — suggesting the beat wasn't large enough or forward guidance wasn't strong enough to sustain the 12% move. Volume running at 2.26x normal confirms a real catalyst, but the magnitude of the close likely reflects short-covering or options activity layered on top of the earnings beat.

RXT dropped 14.3% today on 2x normal volume after a 134% surge over the prior 5 days, with RSI at 75.9 — the move looks like profit-taking and mean reversion following an extreme short-term run-up, with no news catalyst to explain the reversal direction or magnitude.

SATL is up 15.2% today on 1.74x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The 5-day gain of 23.3% and bullish MACD cross suggest sustained buying pressure, but the catalyst — whether options flow, peer rotation, or an undiscovered announcement — is not visible in the available data.

SMTC is up 12.25% today with volume running at 2.74x normal and RSI at 77.4, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The 5-day gain of 27.66% suggests this may be part of a sustained momentum run driven by options flow, sector rotation, or insider/institutional activity not visible in the provided data.

XOM surged 3.67% with no company-specific news to explain the move. The only supplied news covers US-China geopolitical sentiment, which is low-impact and not directly tied to Exxon's fundamentals or oil prices. The move is likely driven by broader energy sector rotation, macro commodity flows, or options activity not visible in this data.

INTC is up 3.6% today and 35% over the past five days with RSI at 85.8 (overbought), but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The sustained surge likely reflects a broader catalyst earlier in the week — possibly M&A speculation, earnings, or sector rotation — that isn't visible in today's news feed; today's increment looks like continuation momentum rather than a fresh news driver.
KO moved +0.34% on below-average volume (relative volume 0.87) with no material news. The move is too small and technically unremarkable to attribute to any specific catalyst.

NVDA is up ~2% today and ~10.5% over the past week, but none of the supplied news items carry high impact or directly explain the move. The 5-day surge is likely driven by broader macro tailwinds — including improving U.S.-China trade sentiment — which could benefit chip exporters like Nvidia, but that link is speculative and not substantiated by any specific Nvidia catalyst in the provided news. The note that Jensen Huang was excluded from Trump's China trip is mildly negative, not a driver of upside.

RKLB is up 11% today and 46% over the past five days on 2.7x normal volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The magnitude and persistence of the rally — combined with overbought RSI at 73 — suggest momentum chasing, options activity, or broader space/defense sector rotation that isn't captured in the available news.
